_meta.description: Machine-readable definitions for operational restriction flags in the Oil Painting Research Assistant. Restriction flags constrain how a chunk or source may be used in retrieval, citation, and answer generation. A chunk may carry multiple flags simultaneously. All flag values must match controlled_vocabulary.restriction_flag.

flags.description: This chunk contains product-specific information only. It must not be used to support claims about general material chemistry or historical practice.
This chunk contains modern information (formulations, products, practices) that must not be cited as historical evidence.
This chunk may be used to inform retrieval ranking and answer assembly but must not be directly cited in answers. Use its content but do not reference the source.
This chunk may be used but citations should be prefaced with a note about the source's limitations (e.g. commercial publisher, unreviewed web content, interpretive text).
flags.note: Does not block citation. Flags that a caveat should accompany it.

flags.description: This chunk is from a case study and must not be generalized. Any claim derived from it must be labeled case_specific and scoped to the specific case.
This chunk or source requires human review before use. It may have quality issues, uncertain metadata, or OCR problems. Must not be served until review is complete.
flags.note: Effective only if approval_state is not_approved or internal_draft_only. A reviewed record that passes review should have this flag removed.

flags.description: This chunk is approved for testing and internal use but may not be served in live user-facing responses. Typically set when content is useful for development but not ready for production.
